fact: In 2005, my galleries hosted 21 exhibitions. The flagship exhibition was 'Hatched: National Graduate Show 2005' (showcasing 60 emerging artists). Other major exhibitions that year were Shiro Takatani's media installations (as part of the Japan-Australia exchange RAPT! project), Richard Giblett's work in the 'New Dystopias' group show, and photographer Tony Nathan's solo exhibition 'Proof of Light' (October 20 – November 13, 2005).

In 2005, PICA's CEO and Director was Sarah Miller. Hannah Mathews served as a key curator on my team (starting her curatorial tenure at PICA from 2005 to 2008), shaping the year's experimental visual arts program alongside guest curators.

Artists who exhibited in 2005 include photographer Tony Nathan (solo exhibition 'Proof of Light'), Shiro Takatani (media arts), Richard Giblett (New Dystopias), and Hatched graduates Tom Halliday, Natalie Preiato, Shannon Lyons, Keumhee Oh, Stephanie Hicks, Alex James, Paul Wotherspoon, Joanne Gerke, Rebecca Jones, and Ryan Nazzari.

My history features key directors: Noel Sheridan (founding director, 1989-1993), Sarah Miller (1993-2006), Amy Barrett-Lennard (2006-2022), and Hannah Mathews (2022-Present). Sarah Wall is currently the Curator.

In 2005, I hosted 13 performance works. My long history of performance includes Joan Jonas' mirror performance 'Mirror Piece I & II' (restaged in 2024 for Bunuru season/Perth Festival), Cassils' performance 'Becoming an Image' in their ALCHEMIC show, Amrita Hepi's 'Monumental' choreographic video work, Laure Prouvost (2025), and Francesca Lasica's 'WHEN I AM NOT THERE' season.

PICA Bar & Cafe operates on my front ground-floor verandah, serving as a popular social hub in the Perth Cultural Centre where artists, curators, and visitors gather for drinks, coffee, and discussions.

I was designed in 1896 in the Federation Free Classical style by architect George Temple Poole, featuring red face-brick, stucco details, a central double-height hall, and a signature landmark clock tower on James Street.

Architects Donaldson and Warn designed my major refurbishment in 1991, converting the former boys' school layout into galleries and a performance space, officially opening as PICA on November 8, 1991.

Opened in 1897 as the Perth Boys' and Girls' School (part of the Perth Central School complex). It was segregated, with boys on the ground floor and girls on the first floor. From 1959 to 1988, I was a branch of Perth Technical College (TAFE) before becoming PICA in 1989.
